title: The Victory at Chi Lang Pass
summary: At the treacherous Chi Lang Pass, our troops cleverly defeated the Ming reinforcements, protecting the achievements of the uprising.

slides.text: In 1427, the Ming Dynasty sent a large reinforcement army to our country under General Lieu Thang. They hoped to save the situation.
slides.imagePrompt: Large army with many flags entering a narrow mountain pass, arrogance of the enemy general, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Le Loi and Nguyen Trai chose Chi Lang Pass - a place with high mountains and deep abysses - as the ambush site. Our troops waited on the mountain slopes.
slides.imagePrompt: Narrow Chi Lang pass with steep mountains, Vietnamese soldiers hidden behind rocks and trees, silence before the battle, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When the enemy reached the middle of the valley, our troops attacked simultaneously. The enemy general Lieu Thang was killed at Ma Yen Hill. The enemy was crushed!
slides.imagePrompt: Action scene in the valley, rocks and arrows falling from the mountains, enemy formation broken, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The victory at Chi Lang Pass shattered the Ming Dynasty's final will to invade, paving the way for our country's return to complete independence.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese soldiers celebrating on the high ground, the sun breaking through the clouds over the mountains, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What characteristic of Chi Lang Pass made the enemy afraid?
options: It was extremely treacherous with high mountains and deep abysses
slides.answer: It was extremely treacherous with high mountains and deep abysses

slides.question: Which enemy general was killed at Ma Yen Hill (Chi Lang)?
